title: How do YOU think is the NGE manga going to end
display_title: How do YOU think is the NGE manga going to end
wikitext:
[]: How do YOU think is the NGE manga going to end?             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Oct 22, 2010 7:46 am I personally think that NGEAsuka´s dad/sperm donor was Fuyutsuki for maximum trolling effect and that it´ll just be EoE from Sadamoto´s-perspective.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Fri Oct 22, 2010 9:13 am Sadamoto has avoided Anno's abstract, disembodied mind-fuckery at every turn, preferring to focus on the real and tangible world of Evangelion. However, he occasionally cannot avoid it, as the story requires it, as is the case with volume 8, which includes the events of episode 20, where Shinji is taken into the Eva. I imagine the events of these last two volumes will be very similar to those depicted in The End of Evangelion, however the final act will probably treat Instrumentality with similar style to Sadmoto's take on episode 20. It will most certainly end with "I need you" on the beach, otherwise, why would he include Asuka strangling Shinji in the hospital room? It most certainly is a setup that will come full circle with Shinji strangling Asuka at the end. I think at this point, I don't think it's a matter of how, it's a matter of when.

[]:             Quote Post by InstrumentalityOne » Fri Oct 22, 2010 9:56 am ^ After the two remaining Rebuilds, where we could get yet ANOTHER end. Probably.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Fri Oct 22, 2010 10:44 am Shinji saves both girls, instrumentality never happens, and they live on happily in respect for Misato's memory. :) .... .___.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Oct 22, 2010 11:04 am I'm really suprised one of the more lighter spinoffs or at least one of the Eva2 Shinji good ends didn't expand on that scenario. (Misato died EoE-style, Asuka was saved because Shinji was able to pilot, Rei checked on Shinji and both decided to live on with the others.) Wasted potentional!! Anyway, I think manga-Instrumentality will tie up Gendo/Shinji (and Rei/Shinji) drama if the ending is somehow A/S-based. But I still want a manga Last B of sorts.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Oct 22, 2010 12:29 pm Related -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=109 I think Rebuild is giving Sadamoto a convenient excuse to delay having to get to the crunch point; I would still not be surprised to see an ambiguous "Congratulations!" style ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Oct 22, 2010 10:17 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I'm really suprised one of the more lighter spinoffs or at least one of the Eva2 Shinji good ends didn't expand on that scenario. (Misato died EoE-style, Asuka was saved because Shinji was able to pilot, Rei checked on Shinji and both decided to live on with the others.) Wasted potentional!!

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Sat Oct 23, 2010 1:17 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: But I still want a manga Last B of sorts.

[]:             Quote Post by AngelNo13Bardiel » Sat Oct 23, 2010 8:14 pm At this point, I'll be amazed if it ends at all . Other than that, yet another "it's-all-up-in-the-air" from me.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 27, 2010 2:28 pm I'll admit it. The "severed arm for manga ending" meme is [s]probably[/s] definitely my fault. AND I'M DAMN PROUD OF IT.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Wed Oct 27, 2010 3:16 pm How the manga will end: 1. Shinji arrives in time to save Asuka. Much MP Eva carnage. 2. Rei cooperates with Gendo and tries to start Third Impact and take him to Yui. 3. Shinji talks GNR out of it; GNR turns back into Lilith and Rei and Gendo go back to normal. 4. Gendo commits suicide. 5. As he exits his Eva, a Seele sniper kills Shinji. 6. Rei calls Instrumentality back on so Shinji can reembody, along with Misato, Kaji, and Gendo, oh, and Toji too, since this is the manga. 7. Much mind effery ensues with the characters learning each other's innermost feelings. 8. While in Instrumentality, Seele learns the same lesson that Kaworu learned from Shinji, that Man's will to survive cannot be defeated. Once everyone reembodies, which takes only a brief time in this ending, they confess their crimes and are executed. 9. Rei leaves Shinji in Asuka's care and vanishes. Or maybe he'll just do the severed arm ending.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Wed Oct 27, 2010 4:16 pm thewayneiac wrote: How the manga will end: 1. Shinji arrives in time to save Asuka. Much MP Eva carnage. 2. Rei cooperates with Gendo and tries to start Third Impact and take him to Yui. 3. Shinji talks GNR out of it; GNR turns back into Lilith and Rei and Gendo go back to normal. 4. Gendo commits suicide. 5. As he exits his Eva, a Seele sniper kills Shinji. 6. Rei calls Instrumentality back on so Shinji can reembody, along with Misato, Kaji, and Gendo, oh, and Toji too, since this is the manga. 7. Much mind effery ensues with the characters learning each other's innermost feelings. 8. While in Instrumentality, Seele learns the same lesson that Kaworu learned from Shinji, that Man's will to survive cannot be defeated. Once everyone reembodies, which takes only a brief time in this ending, they confess their crimes and are executed. 9. ORGY!!!

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Wed Oct 27, 2010 7:21 pm I just wish that Asuka is spared from the mauling. I don't really care if it's S/A ending... actually, considering the fact that manga does not really go into the masochism tango of those two, having a Kitchen & Kimochi Warui scenes in the style of EoE would feel pretty out of place now, let alone any kind of fanficesque ROMANTZU~. However, I have this creepy feeling that Sadamoto is only trolling us by giving some false hope that Shinji could maybe manage to save Asuka... My prediction is 3I with a slightly glorified TV ending.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 27, 2010 9:28 pm @Wayne: I ed. BiQ wrote: However, I have this creepy feeling that Sadamoto is only trolling us by giving some false hope that Shinji could maybe manage to save Asuka...

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Wed Oct 27, 2010 9:37 pm ...now that you put it that way, I have to admit that a not-exactly-small part of me would admire Sadamoto if he had the balls to pull off something like that. Rest of me would hate him very, very, very much...

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Thu Oct 28, 2010 4:47 pm Given the obvious foreshadowing in the revamped version of the hospital scene, I'd be very surprised if the manga ended with anything other than "kimochi warui." I'm honestly not sure if a "Congratulations" ending would even make sense. The manga's more about loss and survivor guilt than accepting oneself.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Thu Oct 28, 2010 5:47 pm It'd be nice if it'll end with something different from the kimochi-warui and the omedeto endings, especially considering from how many years it's going on and the hiatus that it has taken... Yet I don't want a Last-B-like ending @_@

[]:             Quote Post by Tabby-chan » Sun Oct 31, 2010 12:24 am Actually, I'm writing a story that has virtually the same "Instrumentality" ending as Evangelion. It basically covers the last two episodes, the EoE Instrumentality, and all of those other "mind scenes" that go on in the series. All in all, I think that the manga (as well as Rebuild) will basically cover what I just said.

[]:             Quote Post by bladerj » Sun Oct 31, 2010 1:43 pm i'll be surprised if it end at all before i die of old age. i'm half expecting sadamoto to say fuck you and reboot the manga a la movies.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sun Oct 31, 2010 9:31 pm Time for brutal honesty: The manga will definitely NOT have an A/S ending.
